961	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardPushforwardAdj`.** If `F ⊣ G`, then the presheaf-of-modules
962	pushforwards along `F` and `G` are also adjoint. De-sheafification of
963	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:226`), dropping the
964	sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s and the `.val`/`.hom` wrappers. This is the H1 linchpin: applied
965	to the open-immersion adjunction `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base` it gives
966	`pushforward β ⊣ pushforward φ`. -/
967	noncomputable def pushforwardPushforwardAdj : pushforward.{u} φ ⊣ pushforward.{u} ψ where
968	  unit :=
969	    (pushforwardId _).inv ≫ pushforwardNatTrans (𝟙 _) adj.counit ≫
970	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₁)).hom ≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).inv
971	  counit :=
972	    (pushforwardComp _ _).hom ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ adj.unit ≫
973	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₂)).hom ≫ (pushforwardId _).hom
974	  left_triangle_components X := by
975	    ext U x
976	    change (X.presheaf.map (adj.counit.app (F.obj U.unop)).op ≫
977	      X.presheaf.map (F.map (adj.unit.app U.unop)).op) _ = _
978	    dsimp only [id_obj]
979	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.left_triangle_components]
980	    simp
981	  right_triangle_components X := by
982	    ext U x
983	    change (X.presheaf.map (G.map (adj.counit.app U.unop)).op ≫
984	      X.presheaf.map (adj.unit.app (G.obj U.unop)).op) _ = _
985	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.right_triangle_components]
986	    simp
987	
988	end PushforwardAdj
989	
990	section StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars
991	
992	universe v'
993	
994	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C]
995	
996	/-- **A sectionwise-isomorphism morphism of presheaves of modules is an isomorphism.**
997	The inverse is assembled sectionwise via `isoMk` (whose forward naturality is exactly the
998	naturality of the given morphism). -/
999	lemma isIso_of_isIso_app {𝓡 : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} 𝓡}
1000	    (g : M ⟶ N) (h : ∀ U, IsIso (g.app U)) : IsIso g := by
1001	  haveI := h
1002	  have hg : g = (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun U => asIso (g.app U))
1003	      (fun _ _ φ => g.naturality φ)).hom :=
1004	    PresheafOfModules.hom_ext (fun _ => rfl)
1005	  rw [hg]; infer_instance
1006	
1007	variable {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1008	
1009	/-- **`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α` is strong monoidal when `α` is sectionwise
1010	bijective.** The lax tensorator `μ` and unit `ε` (`restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`) are assembled
1011	sectionwise from the `ModuleCat`-level ones, which are isomorphisms for a bijective ground-ring
1012	map (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective`, `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`); hence the
1013	presheaf `μ`/`ε` are sectionwise isos, hence isos (`isIso_of_isIso_app`), and the lax structure
1014	upgrades to strong via `Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal`. This is the **H2** presheaf lift of
1015	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
1016	@[implicit_reducible]
1017	noncomputable def restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective
1018	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
1019	    (hα : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (α.app U).hom) :
1020	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).Monoidal := by
1021	  haveI hε : IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α)) :=
1022	    isIso_of_isIso_app _ (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U))
1023	  haveI hμ : ∀ M₁ M₂, IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) M₁ M₂) :=
1024	    fun M₁ M₂ => isIso_of_isIso_app _
1025	      (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U) (M₁.obj U) (M₂.obj U))
1026	  exact Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal _
1027	
1028	end StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars
1029	
1030	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the internal hom of presheaves of modules
1031	(slice formula): the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)`
1032	
1033	This section builds the FIRST primitive of the sheaf internal-hom / dual block
1034	(blueprint `sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`, the `⊗`-inverse's missing ingredient): the
1035	`R(T)`-module structure on the morphism abelian group `M ⟶ N` of presheaves of
1036	modules over a base category `C` with a **terminal object** `T`, where the scalar
1037	ring is the global ring `R(T)`.
1038	
1039	This is exactly the module attached to each value of the slice internal hom
1040	`ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
1041	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`: over the restricted site (terminal `U`),
1042	the section module over `U` is `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` with its `R(U)`-action. The slice
1043	formula is forced by contravariance of the naive pointwise rule
1044	`U ↦ Hom_{R(U)}(M(U), N(U))`; the module of morphisms of *restricted* objects is
1045	the covariant remedy, and its `R(U)`-module structure is the content here.
1046	
1047	The action is `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f`, where `globalSMul f : N ⟶ N` is the
1048	"multiply by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`" endomorphism: at an object `Y`, with
1049	`R(T) → R(Y)` the canonical map `termRingMap` induced by the unique `Y → T`, it is
1050	scalar multiplication by the image of `f`. Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom
1051	`ihom M N = (M ⟶ N)` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean`) but
1052	nothing for the varying structure sheaf at the `PresheafOfModules` level; this is the
1053	project-local supplement. -/
1054	
1055	namespace InternalHom
1056	
1057	open CategoryTheory Limits
1058	
1059	universe vC uC
1060	
1061	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1062	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
1063	
1064	/-- **The canonical ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` from a terminal object `T`.** For each
1065	object `Y`, the unique morphism `Y.unop → T` (terminality) induces, after `op` and
1066	applying `R`, the ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` along which a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` acts
1067	on `R(Y)`-modules. Project-local: the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` (the slice
1068	internal-hom value) is defined through this map. -/
1069	noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) : R.obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶ R.obj Y :=
1070	  R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op
1071	
1072	/-- **Naturality of `termRingMap`.** The restriction map `R(g) : R(X) → R(Y)` of `R`
1073	along `g : X ⟶ Y` carries `termRingMap X f` to `termRingMap Y f`; equivalently the
1074	images of a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` are compatible with restriction. This is the
1075	sole ingredient making `globalSMul f` a genuine morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
1076	lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
1077	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g))
1078	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
1079	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
1080	  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
1081	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
1082	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map (hT.from X.unop).op)) f) = _
1083	  rw [show termRingMap hT Y = R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op from rfl, ← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl
1084	
1085	/-- **Multiplication by a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` as an endomorphism of `N`.** At an
1086	object `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by `termRingMap Y f ∈ R(Y)` on `N(Y)`; the
1087	naturality square commutes by `termRingMap_naturality` and the semilinearity of the
1088	restriction maps of `N` (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`). This is the central object: it
1089	gives the `R(T)`-action on `Hom(M, N)` by post-composition (`homModule`). -/
1090	noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1091	    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
1092	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
1093	    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
1094	      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))
1095	    (by
1096	      intro X Y g; ext m
1097	      change (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
1098	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) m)
1099	          = (ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) ((LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj X : Type u) (N.obj X)
1100	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)) m)
1101	      rw [LinearMap.lsmul_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
1102	      rw [termRingMap_naturality]; rfl)
1103	
1104	variable {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1105	
1106	/-- The section-wise action of `globalSMul f`: at `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by
1107	`termRingMap Y f`. -/
1108	lemma globalSMul_hom_apply (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) (m : N.obj Y) :
1109	    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl
1110	
1111	/-- `globalSMul 1 = 𝟙`. -/
1112	lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
1113	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_one, one_smul]; rfl
1114	
1115	/-- `globalSMul 0 = 0`. -/
1116	lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
1117	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_zero, zero_smul]; rfl
1118	
1119	/-- `globalSMul` is additive in the scalar. -/
1120	lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1121	    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by
1122	  ext Y m; rw [add_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, _root_.map_add, add_smul]; rfl
1123	
1124	/-- `globalSMul` turns ring multiplication into composition (the scalar endomorphisms
1125	commute, so the order is immaterial). -/
1126	lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1127	    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by
1128	  ext Y m; rw [comp_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, map_mul, mul_smul, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
1129	    LinearMap.comp_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply]
1130	
1131	/-- **The `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` of presheaves of modules over a base
1132	category `C` with a terminal object `T`.** The action `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f` scales
1133	a morphism by post-composing with multiplication by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`; the
1134	module axioms reduce to the `globalSMul_{one,zero,add,mul}` ring-homomorphism facts and
1135	the bilinearity of composition (`Preadditive`). This is the module carried by each value
1136	of the slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1137	(take `C =` the restricted site over `U`, with terminal `U`, so `R(T) = R(U)`).
1138	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom but no `PresheafOfModules`-level
1139	internal hom for the varying structure sheaf. -/
1140	@[implicit_reducible]
1141	noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1142	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
1143	  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1144	  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
1145	  mul_smul f g φ := by
1146	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1147	    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
1148	  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
1149	  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
1150	  smul_add f φ ψ := by
1151	    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1152	    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
1153	  add_smul f g φ := by
1154	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
1155	    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
1156	
1157	/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`
1158	
1159	The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
1160	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom` is realized object-by-object here. The
1161	restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
1162	projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object
1163	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` (`Over.mkIdTerminal`), at which the restricted ring is `R(U)`
1164	(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`
1165	applied to that terminal. -/
1166	
1167	/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
1168	the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
1169	the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
1170	`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
1171	noncomputable def restr (U : C)
1172	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1173	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1174	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M
1175	
1176	/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**
1177	This is the `R(U)`-module underlying `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint
1178	`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions
1179	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U`, equipped with the `homModule` action for the terminal object
1180	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` of `Over U`. The full presheaf `internalHom` (the assembly of these
1181	values over the restriction maps `V ⟶ U`) was assembled iter-220 (`internalHom`). -/
1182	@[implicit_reducible]
1183	noncomputable def internalHomObjModule (U : C)
1184	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1185	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u) (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :=
1186	  homModule (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U)) Over.mkIdTerminal
1187	    (restr U M) (restr U N)
1188	
1189	/-- **The restriction map of the presheaf internal hom.** For a morphism `g : V ⟶ U`
1190	of `C`, restricting a morphism `φ : M|_U ⟶ N|_U` of restricted presheaves of modules
1191	along `Over.map g : Over V ⥤ Over U` yields a morphism `M|_V ⟶ N|_V`. Realised as
1192	`(pushforward₀ (Over.map g) …).map φ`; the target base ring presheaf
1193	`(Over.map g).op ⋙ (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R` is definitionally `(Over.forget V).op ⋙ R`,
1194	so the result has the expected type `restr V M ⟶ restr V N`. This is the
1195	further-restriction map `φ ↦ φ|_V` of blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
1196	noncomputable def restrictionMap {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1197	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1198	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) : restr V M ⟶ restr V N :=
1199	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
1200	    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ
1201	
1202	/-- **`restrictionMap` is additive.** Part of the additivity assertion of blueprint
1203	`lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
1204	lemma restrictionMap_add {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1205	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1206	    (φ ψ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1207	    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
1208	  ext1 X; rfl
1209	
1210	/-- **`restrictionMap` preserves zero.** -/
1211	lemma restrictionMap_zero {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1212	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} :
1213	    restrictionMap g (0 : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) = 0 := by
1214	  ext1 X; rfl
1215	
1216	/-- Helper: the component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two *equal*
1217	objects agrees up to `HEq`. Proven by `subst`. Used to discharge the
1218	pseudofunctoriality coherence of `restrictionMap` (`Over.map` is only functorial in
1219	its argument up to `Over.mapId_eq` / `Over.mapComp_eq`). -/
1220	private lemma hom_app_heq {B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} B}
1221	    (φ : M ⟶ N) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (h : X = Y) : HEq (φ.app X) (φ.app Y) := by
1222	  subst h; rfl
1223	
1224	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: identity.** -/
1225	lemma restrictionMap_id {U : C}
1226	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1227	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1228	    restrictionMap (𝟙 U) φ = φ := by
1229	  ext1 X
1230	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapId_eq]; rfl))
1231	
1232	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: composition.** For `g : V ⟶ U`, `h : W ⟶ V`,
1233	restricting along `h ≫ g` is restricting along `g` then along `h`. -/
1234	lemma restrictionMap_comp {U V W : C} (g : V ⟶ U) (h : W ⟶ V)
1235	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1236	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1237	    restrictionMap (h ≫ g) φ = restrictionMap h (restrictionMap g φ) := by
1238	  ext1 X
1239	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))
1240	
1241	/-- **`restrictionMap` respects composition of morphisms** (functoriality of
1242	`pushforward₀.map`). -/
1243	lemma restrictionMap_comp_hom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1244	    {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1245	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) (ψ : restr U N ⟶ restr U P) :
1246	    restrictionMap g (φ ≫ ψ) = restrictionMap g φ ≫ restrictionMap g ψ :=
1247	  Functor.map_comp _ _ _
1248	
1249	/-- **Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism.** For `g : V ⟶ U`
1250	and a global scalar `r ∈ R(U)`, restricting the multiplication-by-`r` endomorphism of
1251	`N|_U` yields the multiplication-by-`R(g)(r)` endomorphism of `N|_V`. This is the heart
1252	of the semilinearity of `restrictionMap` (blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`):
1253	both sides act on each section by scalar multiplication, and the scalars agree by
1254	functoriality of `R` (`termRingMap_naturality`). -/
1255	lemma restrictionMap_globalSMul {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1256	    {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1257	    (r : (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u)) :
1258	    restrictionMap g (globalSMul (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U))
1259	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr U N) r)
1260	      = globalSMul (R := (Over.forget V).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 V))
1261	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr V N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g.op)) r) := by
1262	  ext1 Y
1263	  ext m
1264	  rw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1265	  erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1266	  congr 1
1267	  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
1268	  have hmor : (Over.forget U).op.map
1269	        (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).op.obj Y).unop).op
1270	      = g.op ≫ (Over.forget V).op.map (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op := by
1271	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
1272	    change Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
1273	      = Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) ≫ g
1274	    have e1 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
1275	        = ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop).hom := by simp
1276	    have e2 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) = Y.unop.hom := by simp
1277	    rw [e1, e2]
1278	    simp [Over.map_obj_hom]
1279	  rw [hmor]
1280	  erw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← R.map_comp]
1281	  rfl
1282	
1283	/-- **`restrictionMap` packaged as an additive group homomorphism** on the morphism
1284	groups, using `restrictionMap_zero` and `restrictionMap_add`. This is the value of the
1285	underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `internalHom`. -/
1286	noncomputable def restrictionMapAddHom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1287	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1288	    (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) →+ (restr V M ⟶ restr V N) where
1289	  toFun := restrictionMap g
1290	  map_zero' := restrictionMap_zero g
1291	  map_add' := restrictionMap_add g
1292	
1293	/-- **The underlying `Ab`-valued presheaf of `internalHom M N`**: `U ↦ (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)`
1294	with the further-restriction maps. Functoriality is `restrictionMap_id` /
1295	`restrictionMap_comp`. This is piece (a)+(b)+(c) of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1296	at the abelian-group level; the `R(U)`-module refinement is added by `internalHom`. -/
1297	noncomputable def internalHomPresheaf
1298	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1299	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC} where
1300	  obj X := AddCommGrpCat.of (restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N)
1301	  map {X Y} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (restrictionMapAddHom f.unop M N)
1302	  map_id X := by
1303	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1304	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1305	    exact restrictionMap_id φ
1306	  map_comp {X Y Z} f f' := by
1307	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1308	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1309	    exact restrictionMap_comp f.unop f'.unop φ
1310	
1311	/-- **Semilinearity of `restrictionMap`** in the form consumed by
1312	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`: for `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`, a global scalar
1313	`r ∈ R(X)` and `m : M|_{X} ⟶ N|_{X}`, `restrictionMap f.unop (r • m) =
1314	R(f)(r) • restrictionMap f.unop m`, where the `•`'s are the slice-value module
1315	actions (`internalHomObjModule`). This is `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`'s
1316	compatibility datum; it follows from `restrictionMap_comp_hom` and
1317	`restrictionMap_globalSMul`. -/
1318	lemma restrictionMap_smul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
1319	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1320	    (r : (R.obj X : Type u)) (m : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N) :
1321	    restrictionMap f.unop
1322	        (letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop M N; r • m)
1323	      = (letI := internalHomObjModule Y.unop M N;
1324	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r • restrictionMap f.unop m) := by
1325	  change restrictionMap f.unop (m ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X.unop N) r)
1326	    = restrictionMap f.unop m
1327	        ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr Y.unop N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r)
1328	  rw [restrictionMap_comp_hom, restrictionMap_globalSMul, Quiver.Hom.op_unop]
1329	
1330	end InternalHom
1331	
1332	/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base
1333	
1334	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
1335	presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
1336	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
1337	category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
1338	site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
1339	hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/
1340	
1341	namespace InternalHom
1342	
1343	section Assembly
1344	
1345	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1346	
1347	/-- **The presheaf internal hom `ℋom(M, N)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`):
1348	the presheaf of `R`-modules assembled from the slice value modules
1349	`internalHomObjModule` (a), the further-restriction maps `internalHomPresheaf` (b)(c),
1350	and the semilinearity datum `restrictionMap_smul`, via `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`.
1351	Stated over a single-universe base (e.g. the topological site `Opens X`); see the
1352	section note for the universe constraint. -/
1353	noncomputable def internalHom
1354	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1355	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1356	  @PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf D _ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
1357	    (internalHomPresheaf M N)
1358	    (fun X => internalHomObjModule X.unop M N)
1359	    (fun {_ _} f r m => restrictionMap_smul f M N r m)
1360	
1361	end Assembly
1362	
1363	end InternalHom
1364	
1365	namespace InternalHom
1366	
1367	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
1368	
1369	universe vC uC
1370	
1371	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1372	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
1373	
1374	/-- **`termRingMap` at the terminal object itself is the identity.** The unique
1375	`T → T` is the identity, so the induced ring map `R(T) → R(T)` is `id`. This is the
1376	fact that the global-scalar action evaluated at the terminal section is the scalar
1377	itself, used to prove the evaluation map of `internalHomEval` is `R(U)`-bilinear. -/
1378	lemma termRingMap_terminal (f : (R.obj (op T) : Type u)) :
1379	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT (op T))) f = f := by
1380	  have hid : hT.from T = 𝟙 T := hT.hom_ext _ _
1381	  simp only [termRingMap, unop_op, hid, op_id, R.map_id]
1382	  rfl
1383	
1384	end InternalHom
1385	
1386	/-! ### The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`
1387	
1388	The presheaf dual is the internal hom into the monoidal unit (the structure presheaf
1389	viewed as a module over itself), assembled in the same single-universe context as
1390	`InternalHom.internalHom`. Blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`. -/
1391	
1392	section Dual
1393	
1394	open InternalHom Opposite
1395	
1396	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1397	
1398	/-- **The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`): the
1399	internal hom into the monoidal unit `𝟙_` (the structure presheaf `R` viewed as a module
1400	over itself / the regular representation). The value
1401	`M^∨(U) = ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ R|_U)` is the `R(U)`-module of `R|_U`-linear
1402	functionals on `M|_U`, the presheaf-of-modules analogue of the fixed-ring linear dual
1403	`Module.Dual R M = (M ⟶ R)` that serves as the `⊗`-inverse of an invertible module.
1404	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring dual but no `PresheafOfModules`-level dual for
1405	the varying structure sheaf. -/
1406	noncomputable def dual
1407	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1408	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1409	  InternalHom.internalHom M (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1410	
1411	/-- The evaluation functional `φ ↦ (s ↦ φ(s))` of a dual section `φ`, cast to an
1412	`R₀.obj X`-linear map `M(X) → R(X)` (the over-category ring at the terminal section is
1413	definitionally `R₀.obj X`). Helper for `internalHomEvalApp`. -/
1414	noncomputable def evalLin
1415	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1416	    (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
1417	    (M.obj X : Type u) →ₗ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] (R₀.obj X : Type u) :=
1418	  ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1419	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
1420	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
1421	
1422	/-- `evalLin` is additive in the dual section `φ` (the dual-section addition is the
1423	categorical `Hom`-addition, on which `app`/`hom`-application is definitionally additive). -/
1424	lemma evalLin_add
1425	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1426	    (φ φ' : (dual M).obj X) :
1427	    evalLin M X (φ + φ') = evalLin M X φ + evalLin M X φ' :=
1428	  LinearMap.ext fun _ => rfl
1429	
1430	/-- `evalLin` is `R₀.obj X`-linear in the dual section `φ`, using the `homModule`
1431	action `c • φ = φ ≫ globalSMul c` and `termRingMap_terminal`. -/
1432	lemma evalLin_smul
1433	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1434	    (c : (R₀.obj X : Type u)) (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
1435	    evalLin M X (c • φ) = c • evalLin M X φ := by
1436	  refine LinearMap.ext fun s => ?_
1437	  rw [LinearMap.smul_apply]
1438	  change ((ConcreteCategory.hom
1439	      (termRingMap (R := (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ R₀) Over.mkIdTerminal
1440	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))) c)
1441	    • ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1442	        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
1443	          (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom s : (R₀.obj X : Type u))
1444	    = c • evalLin M X φ s
1445	  rw [termRingMap_terminal]
1446	  rfl
1447	
1448	/-- **The open-by-open evaluation/contraction map** underlying `internalHomEval`. At an
1449	object `X` it is the `R(X)`-bilinear contraction
1450	`(M(X)) ⊗_{R(X)} (M|_X ⟶ R|_X) → R(X)`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, where `φ(s)` evaluates the
1451	functional `φ` (a morphism of restricted presheaves of modules over `Over X.unop`) at its
1452	terminal section, applied to `s`. Bilinearity over `R(X)`: linearity in `s` is linearity
1453	of `evalLin`; linearity in `φ` is `evalLin_add` / `evalLin_smul`. The codomain is cast to
1454	`R₀.obj X` (defeq to the unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`) to pin the `CommRingCat` module. -/
1455	noncomputable def internalHomEvalApp
1456	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
1457	    (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M)).obj X ⟶
1458	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj X :=
1459	  show ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u)
1460	        (TensorProduct (R₀.obj X : Type u) (M.obj X) ((dual M).obj X))
1461	      ⟶ ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u) (R₀.obj X : Type u) from
1462	  ModuleCat.ofHom
1463	    (TensorProduct.lift
1464	      (LinearMap.mk₂ (R₀.obj X : Type u)
1465	        (fun (s : M.obj X) (φ : (dual M).obj X) => evalLin M X φ s)
1466	        (fun s s' φ => _root_.map_add (evalLin M X φ) s s')
1467	        (fun c s φ => _root_.map_smul (evalLin M X φ) c s)
1468	        (fun s φ φ' => by
1469	          change evalLin M X (φ + φ') s = evalLin M X φ s + evalLin M X φ' s
1470	          rw [evalLin_add, LinearMap.add_apply])
1471	        (fun c s φ => by
1472	          change evalLin M X (c • φ) s = c • evalLin M X φ s
1473	          rw [evalLin_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply])))
1474	
1475	/-- Value of `internalHomEvalApp` on a simple tensor: the contraction `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`.
1476	The eval value is kept at its NATURAL over-ring type `R₀.obj X` (not ascribed to the
1477	unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`). Used to reduce the naturality of `internalHomEval`. -/
1478	@[simp] lemma internalHomEvalApp_tmul
1479	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1480	    (s : (M.obj X : Type u)) (φ : ((dual M).obj X : Type u)) :
1481	    (internalHomEvalApp M X).hom (s ⊗ₜ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] φ) = evalLin M X φ s :=
1482	  rfl
1483	
1484	/-- **Further-restriction of `M|_U` along the canonical `Over.homMk f.unop` equals `M.map f`.**
1485	For `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Dᵒᵖ`, restricting `M|_{X.unop}` (the `pushforward₀` of `M` along
1486	`Over.forget X.unop`) along the over-category morphism `Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶
1487	Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)` is, definitionally, `M.map f`. Extracted as its own lemma so the heavy
1488	`whnf` defeq runs once within its own heartbeat budget (the `internalHomEval` naturality proof
1489	would otherwise time out). Used to rewrite the `naturality_apply` of a dual section into the
1490	shape `ev_M`'s naturality needs. -/
1491	private lemma restr_map_homMk
1492	    (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
1493	    (restr X.unop N).map (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
1494	      = N.map f := rfl
1495	
1496	/-- **The evaluation morphism `ev_M : M ⊗_R M^∨ ⟶ R`** (blueprint `lem:internal_hom_eval`):
1497	the full natural morphism `M ⊗_R M^∨ ⟶ R`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, with `app X := internalHomEvalApp M X`.
1498	CLOSED axiom-clean iter-224 (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`). The per-object/value content
1499	is `internalHomEvalApp` + `internalHomEvalApp_tmul`; the `naturality` field is the six-step
1500	`evalLin`/`key`/`hdt` reduction recorded in the proof body. The iter-222/223 `whnf` HEARTBEAT-BOMB
1501	diagnosis (>200000 heartbeats forced by `kabstract` whnf-ing the monoidal unit `𝟙_`) turned out to be
1502	STALE after a Mathlib update: the composition now splits cleanly with
1503	`erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, …]`, every elementwise rewrite (`internalHomEvalApp_tmul`,
1504	`PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply`, `restr_map_homMk`) fires without any bomb, and the dual-section
1505	naturality square closes directly. No transparency hacks (`with_reducible`), no `unit`-reshape, and no
1506	`maxHeartbeats` bump were needed. -/
1507	noncomputable def internalHomEval
1508	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1509	    PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M) ⟶
1510	      𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
1511	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk (fun X => internalHomEvalApp M X) (by
1512	    intro X Y f
1513	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s φ => ?_)
1514	    -- iter-224: the iter-222/223 `whnf` HEARTBEAT-BOMB diagnosis (the codomain `𝟙_` forcing
1515	    -- `kabstract` to whnf the monoidal-unit machinery, >200000 heartbeats) turned out STALE after a
1516	    -- Mathlib update. The composition splits cleanly with `erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, …]` and every
1517	    -- elementwise rewrite fires without any bomb; the six-step `evalLin`/`key`/`hdt` reduction below
1518	    -- goes through directly. No `with_reducible`, no `unit`-reshape, no `maxHeartbeats` bump.
1519	    -- Step 1: break the two `≫` on `s ⊗ₜ φ`, then `tensorObj_map_tmul` + `internalHomEvalApp_tmul`
1520	    -- (handled by `erw`'s defeq matching through `restrictScalars`):
1521	    --   LHS ⇒ `evalLin M Y ((dual M).map f φ) ((M.map f) s)`.
1522	    erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply,
1523	      internalHomEvalApp_tmul, internalHomEvalApp_tmul]
1524	    simp only []
1525	    -- Reduce the RHS `(𝟙_).map f`-image of `(internalHomEvalApp M X)(s ⊗ₜ φ)` to the unit ring

1527	    change M.evalLin Y ((M.dual.map f) φ) ((M.map f) s)
1528	      = ((𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
1529	          (M.evalLin X φ s)
1530	    -- Step 4: naturality of the dual section `φ` (a morphism over `Over X.unop`) along the
1531	    -- canonical `(Over.homMk f.unop).op : (terminal) ⟶ (op (Over.mk f.unop))`, applied to `s`.
1532	    have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
1533	      (φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1534	        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
1535	      (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op s
1536	    -- Both further-restrictions `(restr X.unop _).map (Over.homMk f.unop).op` are definitionally
1537	    -- the base maps `M.map f` / `(𝟙_).map f` (`restr_map_homMk`).
1538	    rw [restr_map_homMk, restr_map_homMk] at key
1539	    -- Step 5 (`hdt`): identify the dual section's terminal value with `φ` evaluated at
1540	    -- `op (Over.mk f.unop)` (the over-objects `Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)` and `Over.mk f.unop`
1541	    -- are equal via `Category.id_comp`, with equal source `Y.unop`, so `hom_app_heq`+`eq_of_heq`).
1542	    have hdt : M.evalLin Y ((M.dual.map f) φ) = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
1543	      congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom
1544	        (eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq
1545	          (φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1546	            (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
1547	          (congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop)))))
1548	    -- Step 6: chain `hdt` (applied to `(M.map f) s`) into `key`.
1549	    exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key
1550	    )

1552	/-- **Precomposition `R(U)`-linear equivalence on dual sections induced by an iso `e : M ≅ M'`.**
1553	At an object `U`, sends a dual section `φ : M'|_U ⟶ R|_U` to `(e.hom|_U) ≫ φ : M|_U ⟶ R|_U`, with
1554	inverse `ψ ↦ (e.inv|_U) ≫ ψ`. The `R(U)`-action on dual sections is post-composition with
1555	`globalSMul` (the `homModule` action), which commutes with this pre-composition, so the map is
1556	`R(U)`-linear. This is the section-level core of `dualIsoOfIso` (the dual is contravariantly
1557	functorial in isomorphisms). -/
1558	noncomputable def dualPrecompEquiv
1559	    {M M' : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} (e : M ≅ M') (U : D) :
1560	    letI := internalHomObjModule U M'
1561	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1562	    letI := internalHomObjModule U M
1563	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1564	    (restr U M' ⟶ restr U (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
1565	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op U) : Type u)]
1566	      (restr U M ⟶ restr U (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) := by
1567	  letI := internalHomObjModule U M'
1568	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1569	  letI := internalHomObjModule U M
1570	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1571	  exact
1572	    { toFun := fun φ =>
1573	        (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1574	          (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.hom ≫ φ
1575	      invFun := fun ψ =>
1576	        (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1577	          (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv ≫ ψ
1578	      map_add' := fun φ ψ => Preadditive.comp_add _ _ _ _ φ ψ
1579	      map_smul' := fun r φ => by
1580	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
1581	        exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
1582	      left_inv := fun φ => by
1583	        change (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1584	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv
1585	          ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1586	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.hom ≫ φ) = φ
1587	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, e.inv_hom_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
1588	          Category.id_comp]
1589	      right_inv := fun ψ => by
1590	        change (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1591	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.hom
1592	          ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1593	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv ≫ ψ) = ψ
1594	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, e.hom_inv_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
1595	          Category.id_comp] }
1596	
1597	/-- **The presheaf dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
1598	`e : M ≅ M'` of presheaves of modules induces an isomorphism `dual M' ≅ dual M` of their
1599	duals (`dual N = internalHom N (𝟙_)`), assembled sectionwise from the precomposition
1600	equivalences `dualPrecompEquiv`. This is the `\leanok`-ready ingredient that transports a
1601	local trivialisation `L|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` to `dual (L|_U) ≅ dual 𝒪_U` in the assembly of

1619	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
1620	
1621	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1622	
1623	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1624	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1625	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1626	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1627	
1628	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
1629	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1630	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1631	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1632	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1633	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1634	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1635	
1636	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
1637	
1638	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1639	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1640	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1641	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1642	
1643	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1644	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1645	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1646	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1647	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1648	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1649	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1650	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1651	
1652	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1653	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1654	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1655	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1656	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1657	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1658	law is definitional. -/
1659	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1660	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1661	
1662	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
1663	
1664	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
1665	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
1666	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
1667	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
1668	
1669	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
1670	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
1671	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
1672	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
1673	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
1674	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
1675	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
1676	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
1677	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
1678	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
1679	
1680	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
1681	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
1682	gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).
1683	
1684	Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
1685	tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
1686	`⊗`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
1687	noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1688	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1689	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
1690	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1691	
1692	/-- **The sheaf-level dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
1693	`e : M ≅ M'` in `X.Modules` induces `dual M' ≅ dual M`, obtained by sheafifying the presheaf-level
1694	dual iso `PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso` of the underlying presheaf isomorphism. This is the
1695	reusable "dual respects isos" ingredient (the dual analogue of `tensorObjIsoOfIso`) feeding the
1696	assembly of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`: a trivialisation `L.restrict f ≅ 𝒪` yields, contravariantly,
1697	`dual 𝒪 ≅ dual (L.restrict f)`. -/
1698	noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules} (e : M ≅ M') :
1699	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
1700	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1701	    (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (R₀ := X.presheaf)
1702	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e))
1703	
1704	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
1705	
1706	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
1707	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
1708	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
1709	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
1710	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
1711	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
1712	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
1713	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
1714	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1715	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
1716	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/

1718	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
1719	
1720	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
1721	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
1722	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the

1725	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
1726	
1727	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
1728	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
1729	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
1730	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
1731	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
1732	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1733	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1734	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
1735	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1736	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1737	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1738	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
1739	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))

1742	sheaf.**

1746	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly

1748	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
1749	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :

1754	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app

1889	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}

1892	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
1893	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU

1913	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
1914	
1915	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
1916	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
1917	
1918	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
1919	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
1920	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
1921	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
1922	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
1923	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
1924	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
1925	  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
1926	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.
1927	  Step 4: close that presheaf goal by **H1 ∘ H2**:
1928	    • H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient, BUILT this iter): the presheaf-level iso
1929	      `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
1930	      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
1931	      pair `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
1932	      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
1933	      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`
1934	      definitionally.
1935	    • H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`
1936	      with `β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars β`

1938	      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{μ,ε}`); the composite
1939	      `μIso` is the tensorator.

1941	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
1942	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
1943	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
1944	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
1945	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
1946	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
1947	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
1948	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
1949	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
1950	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
1951	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
1952	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
1953	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
1954	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
1955	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
1956	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
1957	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
1958	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
1959	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
1960	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
1961	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
1962	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
1963	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
1964	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
1965	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
1966	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
1967	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
1968	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL CLOSURE — iter-217 H1 build). The remaining presheaf goal is
1969	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
1970	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
1971	  -- where `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
1972	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
1973	  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, built from
1974	  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
1975	  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
1976	  --      open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`, so
1977	  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally.
1978	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅
1979	  --      (pushforward β).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β).obj B`.
1980	  -- `φR` (the scheme structure map) and `β` (the restrictFunctor structure map) are kept as
1981	  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the
1982	  -- open-immersion adjunction is INLINED for the same reason (a `have` would make `adj.unit`
1983	  -- opaque and block the `congr` defeq, exactly as in Mathlib's sheaf-level `restrictAdjunction`).
1984	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
1985	  -- The restrictFunctor structure map `β` (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`).
1986	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
1987	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
1988	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
1989	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
1990	  -- H1 via the presheaf pushforward-pushforward adjunction + `leftAdjointUniq`.
1991	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
1992	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
1993	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
1994	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
1995	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
1996	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
1997	  -- H2: the strong-monoidal tensorator of `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`.
1998	  -- `β` is sectionwise bijective (it is the `forget₂`-image of the open-immersion structure ring
1999	  -- ISO `f.appIso`), so `restrictScalars β` is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`),
2000	  -- and `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` is `Monoidal` (Mathlib); the composite's `μIso` is the tensorator.
2001	  -- It is built over the SYNTACTIC `_ ⋙ forget₂` base form (where the `MonoidalCategory` instance is
2002	  -- found canonically); the result is DEFEQ to the goal — whose base `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` is only
2003	  -- defeq, not syntactically, `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂` — and `(pushforward β).obj M.val =
2004	  -- (M.restrict f).val` definitionally, so `exact` closes it without any instance diamond.
2005	  have hβ : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (β.app U).hom := by
2006	    intro U
2007	    haveI : IsIso (β.app U) :=
2008	      inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (f.appIso U.unop).inv))
2009	    exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (β.app U)

2011	      (f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := β

2013	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective β' hβ

2015	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf
2016	      ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β')

2020	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
2021	
2022	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
2023	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
2024	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
2025	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
2026	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
2027	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
2028	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
2029	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
2030	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
2031	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
2032	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
2033	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
2034	  intro x
2035	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
2036	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
2037	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
2038	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
2039	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
2040	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
2041	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
2042	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
2043	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
2044	    tensorObj_unit_iso

2046	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)
2047	
2048	The "locally-iso ⇒ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:

2052	in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
2053	colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
2054	open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
2055	statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/

2057	/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
2058	an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
2059	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
2060	is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
2061	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
2062	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
2063	    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)
2064	    (h : ∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)) :
2065	    IsIso φ := by
2066	  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
2067	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
2068	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
2069	    intro x
2070	    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
2071	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
2072	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
2073	      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
2074	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
2075	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).
2076	    haveI hFφ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙
2077	        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by
2078	      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
2079	    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).ι x' = x`.
2080	    have hGφ : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} ((U x).ι x')).map
2081	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) :=
2082	      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
2083	        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ
2084	    exact hx' ▸ hGφ
2085	  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
2086	  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
2087	  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
2088	  let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
2089	  haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := hst
2090	  haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
2091	  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := by
2092	    have := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
2093	    exact this
2094	  exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1

2096	/-- **A-bridge step (ii): promote an `𝒪_X`-linear `Ab`-presheaf morphism to a module
2097	morphism.** Given a morphism `g : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf` of the underlying
2098	`Ab`-presheaves that is sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linear, package it as a morphism `M ⟶ N`
2099	of `𝒪_X`-modules. This wraps `PresheafOfModules.homMk` at the `Scheme.Modules` level;
2100	it is the "promote to `𝒪_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`
2101	(the ab-sheaf gluing produces the linear `g`; this lemma turns it into a module map).
2102	Sectionwise linearity is a property the consumer checks on a separated presheaf. -/
2103	noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
2104	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
2105	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
2106	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
2107	    M ⟶ N :=
2108	  ⟨PresheafOfModules.homMk (M₁ := M.val) (M₂ := N.val) g hg⟩

2110	/-- The underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `homMk g hg` is `g` itself. -/
2111	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map_homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
2112	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
2113	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
2114	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
2115	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map (homMk g hg) = g :=

2188	lemma exists_tensorObj_inverse {X : Scheme.{u}} {L : X.Modules}

2264	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site
2265	equivalence (the SHARED root of both ⊗-inverse bridges)
2266	
2267	For a topological space `X` and an open `U : Opens X`, Mathlib supplies the
2268	1-categorical equivalence `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
2269	(`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`) but leaves, as a documented `## TODO`, the
2270	upgrade of this to an *equivalence of sheaf categories*
2271	
2272	  `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
2273	
2274	This is the single Mathlib-absent root on which BOTH remaining ⊗-inverse bridges
2275	(the A-engine `homOfLocalCompat` gluing and the C-bridge `dual_isLocallyTrivial`)
2276	reduce: re-evaluating a slice-internal-hom along the open immersion `U ↪ X` is
2277	exactly transport across this sheaf-site equivalence. It is value-category
2278	parametric (`A` is arbitrary), so one build serves both lanes.
2279	
2280	The proof routes through `CategoryTheory.Equivalence.sheafCongr`, which needs only
2281	the dense-subsite instance `(overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite …`. The sole
2282	non-formal content of that instance is the cover-correspondence
2283	`functorPushforward_mem_iff`, which we discharge *pointwise*: on the thin poset
2284	`Opens X` a covering sieve is a pointwise neighbourhood cover, and the open
2285	embedding `↥U ↪ X` matches points and opens on both sides. No `Over.map`
2286	pseudofunctor coherence appears — thinness trivialises it.
2287	
2288	Per blueprint `lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv`. -/
2289	
2290	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
2291	
2292	section OverSliceSheafEquiv
2293	
2294	open Topology CategoryTheory
2295	
2296	universe v w
2297	
2298	-- NOTE: within the `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` namespace the unqualified
2299	-- identifier `Opens` resolves to the scheme-theoretic `Scheme.Opens` (expecting a
2300	-- `Scheme`), shadowing the intended point-set `TopologicalSpace.Opens`. We therefore
2301	-- fully qualify every `TopologicalSpace.Opens …` here; `Over`/`Sieve`/`GrothendieckTopology`
2302	-- are `CategoryTheory` names with no scheme shadow and stay unqualified.
2303	
2304	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
2305	
2306	/-- Pointwise cover-correspondence underlying `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`: a
2307	sieve `S` on `W : Opens ↥U` is a covering sieve in the subspace topology iff its
2308	pushforward along the open-embedding image functor `↥U ↪ X` is a covering sieve in
2309	`X`. Both sides are the pointwise neighbourhood-cover condition of
2310	`Opens.grothendieckTopology`, matched across the injection `Subtype.val`. -/
2311	private lemma overEquiv_image_cover_iff (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥U) (S : Sieve W) :
2312	    (S.functorPushforward ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U))
2313	        ∈ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
2314	          (((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W)
2315	      ↔ S ∈ (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) W := by
2316	  constructor
2317	  · intro h y hy
2318	    have hx : (y : X) ∈
2319	        (((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W) :=
2320	      ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
2321	    obtain ⟨V, f, hf, hxV⟩ := h y hx
2322	    obtain ⟨W', a, b, hSa, _⟩ := hf
2323	    refine ⟨W', a, hSa, ?_⟩
2324	    have hyim : (y : X) ∈
2325	        ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W' := b.le hxV
2326	    obtain ⟨z, hz, hzeq⟩ := hyim
2327	    rw [← (Subtype.ext hzeq : z = y)]; exact hz
2328	  · intro h x hx
2329	    obtain ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩ := hx
2330	    obtain ⟨W', a, hSa, hyW'⟩ := h y hy
2331	    refine ⟨((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W',
2332	      ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).map a, ?_,
2333	      ⟨y, hyW', rfl⟩⟩
2334	    exact ⟨W', a, 𝟙 _, hSa, by simp⟩
2335	
2336	/-- The inverse of `overEquivalence U` exhibits `(Opens ↥U, subspace topology)` as a
2337	dense subsite of `(Over U, slice topology)`. This is the dense-subsite datum
2338	`Equivalence.sheafCongr` consumes; the only non-formal field is the pointwise
2339	cover-correspondence `overEquiv_image_cover_iff`. -/
2340	instance overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite :
2341	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
2342	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
2343	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
2344	  functorPushforward_mem_iff {W S} := by
2345	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
2346	    rw [show Sieve.overEquiv ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W)
2347	          (S.functorPushforward (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse)
2348	        = S.functorPushforward
2349	            ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U) by
2350	      rw [Sieve.functorPushforward_comp]; rfl]
2351	    exact overEquiv_image_cover_iff U W S
2352	
2353	/-- **The open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site equivalence.** For a topological space
2354	`X`, an open `U : Opens X`, and any value category `A`, the slice sheaf category
2355	over `U` is equivalent to the sheaf category on the open subspace `↥U`:
2356	
2357	  `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
2358	
2359	This completes the documented Mathlib `## TODO` at `Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` and
2360	is the SHARED root both ⊗-inverse bridges (`homOfLocalCompat`, `dual_isLocallyTrivial`)
2361	reduce to. Built by transporting `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U` across
2362	`CategoryTheory.Equivalence.sheafCongr`, using the project-local dense-subsite
2363	instance `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`.
2364	
2365	Per blueprint `lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv`. -/
2366	noncomputable def overSliceSheafEquiv (A : Type w) [Category.{v} A] :
2367	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌
2368	      Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
2369	  (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
2370	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U)
2371	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
2372	
2373	end OverSliceSheafEquiv
2374	
2375	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

8	/-!
9	# Flattening stratification of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem — the technical heart that
14	turns the absolute Quot-functor representability problem on a projective
15	morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with coherent input `𝓕` into a finite collection of
16	locally-closed strata over which `𝓕` becomes flat.
17	
18	## Status (iter-176 Lane E file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
19	
20	iter-175 Lane E died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
21	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
22	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the
23	*intended* substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
24	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a
25	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
26	deep (Nitsure~\S4 ~250-line argument: generic-flatness ⟶ Noetherian
27	induction ⟶ Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity ⟶ assembly via direct images
28	`E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`).
29	
30	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat` (def, ~8 LOC) — the
33	   **coherent-sheaf flatness predicate** for a coherent `𝓕` on `X` over
34	   `S` via `f : X ⟶ S`. Affine-locally: for `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ f⁻¹U` both
35	   affine, `Γ(𝓕, V)` is a flat `Γ(S, U)`-module via the composite
36	   `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V) → End(Γ(𝓕, V))`. The corresponding Stacks tag is
37	   00HB (module-level) lifted to a sheaf-level predicate by the
38	   affine-local descent characterisation.
39	
40	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem, ~15 LOC) — **generic
41	   flatness** [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base
42	   `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a
43	   non-empty open `V ⊆ S`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification` (theorem, ~25 LOC) — the
46	   **flattening-stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4]: for `S`
47	   noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there exists a
48	   finite locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (set-theoretically
49	   covering, pairwise disjoint) such that `𝓕` becomes flat on each
50	   stratum.
51	
52	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification_universal` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
53	   — the **universal property** of the stratification [Nitsure §4 (ii)]:
54	   any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that `𝓕` pulled back to `X ×_S T` is
55	   `T`-flat factors uniquely through the stratum coproduct
56	   `i : ⨿ S_f ⟶ S`.
57	
58	Additionally, three sub-lemmas decompose the existence proof, plus a
59	curve-specialisation corollary feeding the Route~A consumer:
60	
61	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
62	   **special case `n = 0`** [Nitsure §4, n=0]: a coherent `𝓕` on `S`
63	   admits a finite locally-closed stratification by integer "rank"
64	   strata.
65	
66	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusReduction` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
67	   **Noetherian-induction reduction** [Nitsure §4 general case opening]:
68	   a finite reduced locally-closed stratification on which `𝓕`
69	   *already* becomes flat (without polynomial indexing).
70	
71	7. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusAssembly` (lemma, ~15 LOC) — the
72	   **assembly step** [Nitsure §4 via direct images]: combining the
73	   `n = 0` stratification iteratively on the direct images
74	   `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)` to refine to the Hilbert-polynomial-indexed
75	   stratification.
76	
77	8. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
78	   — the **relative-curve specialisation** for the Route~A consumer
79	   `C ×_k T → T`.
80	
81	## Note on type expressivity
82	
83	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
84	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
85	
86	- `CoherentSheafFlat` reduces to module-theoretic flatness of every
87	  affine-local section module over the corresponding affine-base ring
88	  via the `appLE` ring-hom — non-empty content because flatness of a
89	  module is the standard non-trivial homological condition.
90	- `genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V`
91	  and substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers
92	  are necessary (without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
93	- `flatteningStratification` packages a *finite* indexed family of
94	  *locally-closed-immersions* `ι : S_f ⟶ S` that pairwise *cover*
95	  `|S|` *disjointly* AND on which the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` becomes
96	  `S_f`-flat — all four conjuncts are substantive (any single
97	  one trivialises if dropped).
98	- `flatteningStratification_universal` packages the universal property
99	  via `∃!` (unique existence of factorisation), a substantive Yoneda
100	  bijection content.
101	
102	## Mathlib status
103	
104	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`,
105	`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat` (morphism-level), `IsImmersion`,
106	`IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`. It does NOT
107	provide: a relative projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, an `IsProjective` morphism
108	property, the flattening-stratification construction, or coherent-sheaf
109	flatness as a sheaf-level predicate.
110	
111	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
112	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
113	restriction is harmless in the Route~A consumer setting where `π` comes
114	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
115	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
116	property, the hypothesis tightens.
117

122	"Construction of Hilbert and Quot schemes", §4 (FGA Explained Ch. 5,
123	arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–18); Stacks Project tags 00HB (module flat),
124	052H (flattening stratification).

133	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

135	namespace Scheme
