137	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
138	
139	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
140	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
141	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
142	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
143	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
144	
145	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
146	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
147	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
148	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
149	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
152	
153	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
154	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
155	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
156	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
157	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
158	morphism of `f`).
159	
160	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
161	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
162	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
163	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
164	the flattening-stratification theorem.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
167	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
168	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
169	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
170	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
171	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
172	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
173	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
174	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
175	
176	end Scheme
177	
178	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
179	
180	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
181	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
182	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
183	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
184	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
185	
186	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
187	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
188	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
189	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
190	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
191	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
192	
193	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
194	
195	/-- **Generic flatness theorem** (Nitsure §4 / Stacks ?).
196	
197	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
198	and a coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a non-empty open subscheme
199	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
200	
201	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine open
202	`Spec A ⊆ S` where `A` is a noetherian domain, then apply the algebraic
203	form (Noether normalisation + Auslander–Buchsbaum-style filtration
204	argument) to each finite-type-`A`-algebra `B` arising from an affine
205	cover of `p⁻¹(Spec A)`. The witness `V` is the common basic open
206	`D(f_1 f_2 ⋯ f_r) ⊆ Spec A` clearing the finitely many
207	generic-flatness elements `f_i ∈ A` produced on each patch. -/
208	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
209	    (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules) :
210	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
211	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
212	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
213	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
214	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
215	  sorry
216	
217	/-! ## §3. Sub-lemmas of the existence proof
218	
219	The Nitsure §4 proof of the existence theorem decomposes into three
220	sub-lemmas wired together by Noetherian induction. The first is the
221	special case `n = 0` (when `ℙⁿ_S = S`); the second is the
222	Noetherian-induction reduction to a finite stratification where `𝓕`
223	*already* becomes flat (but without polynomial indexing); the third is
224	the assembly step that re-introduces the Hilbert-polynomial indexing
225	via the direct images `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`.
226	
227	Blueprint references: `lem:flat_locus_open`, `lem:nonflat_locus_proper`,
228	`lem:noetherian_induction_strata`. -/
229	
230	/-- **Lemma 5 (special case `n = 0`).** [Nitsure §4 special case]
231	
232	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module, there exists a
233	countable family `S_e ⊆ S` (indexed by `e ∈ ℕ`) of locally-closed
234	subschemes such that
235	- each `S_e` is a (locally-closed) immersion `S_e ⟶ S`;
236	- the underlying sets `|S_e|` partition `|S|`;
237	- the pullback `𝓕|_{S_e}` is flat over `𝓞_{S_e}` (an `𝓞_{S_e}`-module
238	  pulled back is automatically `𝓞_{S_e}`-flat over itself, which is the
239	  substantive content for this lemma; the *rank*-`e` refinement is
240	  iter-177+ work once the locally-free-of-rank-`e` predicate is in
241	  scope).
242	
243	The function `s ↦ dim_{κ(s)} 𝓕|_s` is upper-semicontinuous (this is
244	content (iii) of Nitsure's special case), encoded indirectly by the
245	fact that the strata are locally-closed and disjoint.
246	
247	Nitsure's proof: Nakayama produces a local presentation
248	`𝓞_V^{⊕m} ⟶ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} ⟶ 𝓕|_V ⟶ 0`; the closed subscheme `V_e ⊆ V`
249	defined by the matrix-entry ideal of the first map represents the
250	locus of locally-free-of-rank-`e`; gluing over a cover and intersecting
251	with `V ∖ ⋃_{e' > e} V_{e'}` produces the locally-closed `S_e`. -/
252	lemma flatLocusStratification {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
253	    (F : S.Modules) :
254	    ∃ (S_ : ℕ → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ e, S_ e ⟶ S),
255	      (∀ e, IsImmersion (ι e)) ∧
256	      (∀ e e', e ≠ e' → Disjoint (Set.range (ι e).base) (Set.range (ι e').base)) ∧
257	      (∀ s : S, ∃ e, s ∈ Set.range (ι e).base) ∧
258	      (∀ e, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (ι e) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ι e)).obj F)) := by
259	  sorry
260	
261	/-- **Lemma 6 (Noetherian-induction reduction).** [Nitsure §4 general
262	case opening]
263	
264	For `S` noetherian, `π : X ⟶ S` proper, and `𝓕` coherent on `X`, there
265	exist finitely many reduced locally-closed immersions `V_i ⟶ S` with
266	pairwise disjoint set-theoretic images covering `|S|`, such that the
267	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S V_i}` is flat over `V_i`.
268	
269	Nitsure's proof: peel off non-empty open flat patches by
270	`genericFlatness` applied to each irreducible component (with its
271	reduced subscheme structure), induct on the closed complement
272	`S ∖ V` (which is again noetherian, hence the induction terminates by
273	Noetherianity).
274	
275	The substantive content over the special case (Lemma 5) is that the
276	strata `V_i` are *reduced* and that `𝓕` becomes flat above them (not
277	merely that strata exist); the polynomial-indexed refinement of the
278	main theorem (`flatteningStratification`) requires further assembly
279	(`flatLocusAssembly`). -/
280	lemma flatLocusReduction {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
281	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
282	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (V_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ i, V_ i ⟶ S),
283	      (∀ i, IsImmersion (ι i)) ∧
284	      (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (Set.range (ι i).base) (Set.range (ι j).base)) ∧
285	      (∀ s : S, ∃ i, s ∈ Set.range (ι i).base) ∧
286	      (∀ i, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι i))
287	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι i))).obj F)) := by
288	  sorry
289	
290	/-- **Lemma 7 (assembly via direct images).** [Nitsure §4 assembly step]
291	
292	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there
293	exists an integer `N` such that iterating the `n=0` stratification
294	(Lemma 5) on the direct images `E_i := π_*𝓕(N+i)` on `S`
295	(`i = 0, 1, …`) produces, after finitely many refinements, the
296	Hilbert-polynomial-indexed stratification of the main theorem.
297	
298	The substantive content is the existence of the uniform vanishing
299	bound `N` together with the finite refinement chain producing the
300	locally-closed strata `S_f` from the rank-strata `W_{e_0, …, e_n}`.
301	
302	For the iter-176 file-skeleton this is encoded by the existence of a
303	finite locally-closed stratification refining Lemma 6, together with
304	the assertion that the strata carry constant Hilbert polynomial (the
305	"polynomial-locally-constant" content of Nitsure's statement (A)).
306	The Hilbert polynomial itself is encoded abstractly as a function
307	`I → (ℕ → ℤ)` (each numerical polynomial restricted to `ℕ`); the
308	substantive refinement to `numericalPolynomial` of degree `≤ n` is
309	iter-177+ work. -/
310	lemma flatLocusAssembly {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
311	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
312	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
313	      (P : I → ℕ → ℤ),
314	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
315	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
316	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
317	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
318	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) ∧
319	      (∀ f g, f = g ↔ P f = P g) := by
320	  sorry
321	
322	/-! ## §4. Existence of the flattening stratification (Nitsure §4 main)
323	
324	The main theorem combines the three sub-lemmas above: starting from a
325	proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S` and a coherent sheaf `𝓕`, one obtains a
326	finite locally-closed stratification of `S` indexed by Hilbert
327	polynomials, on each piece of which `𝓕` becomes flat, with all four
328	properties of Nitsure's Theorem packaged in a single conjunction.
329	
330	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_exists`
331	(Nitsure §4; Stacks 052H). -/
332	
333	/-- **Flattening stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4 main /
334	Stacks 052H].
335	
336	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S`, and a
337	coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a finite locally-closed
338	stratification `{S_f}` of `S` indexed by a finite set `I` such that
339	- each `ι : S_f ⟶ S` is a (locally-closed) immersion;
340	- the underlying sets `|S_f|` partition `|S|` (disjoint and covering);
341	- the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` is flat over `S_f` for each `f`.
342	
343	The (intended) substantive refinement is that the index set `I` is in
344	bijection with the set of Hilbert polynomials arising on fibres, and
345	that each `S_f` is uniquely determined by its Hilbert polynomial. For
346	the iter-176 file-skeleton the substantive type captures the
347	stratification + flatness; the Hilbert-polynomial labeling is encoded
348	in `flatLocusAssembly`'s `P : I → ℕ → ℤ` injection but elided here for
349	type-clarity.
350	
351	iter-177+: the body assembles `flatLocusReduction` (Lemma 6) ⟶
352	`flatLocusAssembly` (Lemma 7). The structural skeleton is encoded
353	directly here; the Mathlib-side ingredients required (relative
354	projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity, direct image
355	base-change `R^r π_* 𝓕(m) = 0` for `m ≫ 0`, cohomology-and-base-change
356	Stacks tag 02KH for the `H^0` form) are itemised in the blueprint
357	chapter §`Mathlib status`. -/
358	theorem flatteningStratification {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
359	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
360	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),
361	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
362	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
363	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
364	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
365	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) := by
366	  sorry
367	
368	/-! ## §5. Universal property of the stratification
369	
370	Nitsure's part (ii): the morphism `i : ⨿_f S_f → S` assembled from the
371	stratum inclusions is universal for "`𝓕` becomes `T`-flat after pullback
372	along `T → S`". Equivalently, for any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that
373	`𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat, `φ` factors uniquely through some `S_f`.
374	
375	For the iter-176 file-skeleton we encode the universal property in the
376	factor-through form (∃! factorisation through one of the strata
377	`ι f : S_f ⟶ S`), which is the substantive Yoneda-bijection content of
378	Nitsure's part (ii).
379	
380	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_universal`
381	(Nitsure §4 (ii); Stacks 052H). -/
382	
383	/-- **Universal property of the flattening stratification**
384	[Nitsure §4 (ii) / Stacks 052H].
385	
386	Given the flattening stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (existence supplied
387	by `flatteningStratification`), a morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that the
388	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat factors uniquely through one of the
389	locally-closed inclusions `ι : S_f ⟶ S`.
390	
391	The hypothesis takes the stratification data `(I, S_, ι)` as an
392	explicit parameter (rather than re-extracting it from
393	`flatteningStratification`'s existential output) so the universal
394	property is decoupled from the choice of stratification — different
395	witnesses agree on the universal-property content. iter-177+: refine
396	to a `Functor.RepresentableBy`-style universal arrow once the
397	contravariant functor `T ↦ {φ : T ⟶ S | 𝓕|_{X_T} is T-flat}` is
398	available. -/
399	theorem flatteningStratification_universal {S X : Scheme.{u}}
400	    [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules)
401	    (I : Type u) [Finite I] (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
402	    [∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)] :
403	    ∀ {T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ S),
404	      Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π φ)
405	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π φ)).obj F) →
406	      ∃! (data : (f : I) × (T ⟶ S_ f)),
407	        data.2 ≫ ι data.1 = φ := by
408	  sorry
409	
410	/-! ## §6. Curve specialisation (Route A consumer)
411	
412	In the Route A consumer setting, the input morphism is the second
413	projection `pr_T : C ×_k T ⟶ T` of the pullback of a smooth proper
414	curve `C/k` against a noetherian `k`-scheme `T`. A smooth proper curve
415	over a field is automatically projective (Hartshorne II.6.7 / II.7.6;
416	folklore), hence in particular proper; the relative-curve `C ×_k T → T`
417	inherits properness by base change, hence the hypotheses of the main
418	theorem are met. This corollary records the specialisation
419	explicitly. -/
420	
421	/-- **Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative
422	curve** [Nitsure §4 corollary; Route~A consumer A.2.a entry-point].
423	
424	Let `k` be a field, `C` a smooth proper curve over `k` (encoded as
425	`C : Over (Spec k)` with `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` and
426	`[IsProper C.hom]`), and `T` a noetherian `k`-scheme. For any coherent
427	sheaf `𝓕` on the relative curve `C ×_k T`, the flattening
428	stratification conclusion of `flatteningStratification` applies to
429	`π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` and `𝓕`: there is a finite locally-closed
430	stratification `{T_f ⊆ T}` set-theoretically covering `T` disjointly,
431	such that `𝓕|_{C ×_k T_f}` is flat over `T_f` for each `f`.
432	
433	iter-177+: the body invokes `flatteningStratification` on the
434	base-changed morphism `pullback.snd C.hom T.hom : (C ×_k T) ⟶ T`,
435	using that `IsProper (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom)` holds by base change
436	of `IsProper C.hom`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a
437	typed `sorry`. -/
438	theorem flatteningStratification.ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k]
439	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
440	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
441	    (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsLocallyNoetherian T.left]
442	    (F : (Limits.pullback C.hom T.hom).Modules) :
443	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (T_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, T_ f ⟶ T.left),
444	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
445	      (∀ t : T.left, ∃ f, t ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
446	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
447	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat
448	        (pullback.snd (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))
449	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
450	          (pullback.fst (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))).obj F)) := by
451	  sorry
452

10	/-!
11	# `Pic⁰_{C/k}` as an abelian variety (A.3.iii–vii)
12	
13	This file is the **iter-193 Pic0AbelianVariety** file-skeleton (NEW lane,
14	blueprint-doctor flagged orphan resolution for the iter-192 plan-phase chapter
15	`Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`). It packages the strategy phase A.3.iii–vi
16	substrate and the A.3.vii assembly into the load-bearing Route~A statement
17	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety, the gate of `thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness`
18	for `g(C) ≥ 1` curves.
19	
20	The chapter consumes the abstract identity-component substrate set up in
21	`Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` (`Pic⁰_{C/k}` as a clopen subgroup scheme of
22	finite type, geometrically irreducible, formation commuting with base change)
23	and adds the curve-specific input: the tangent-space isomorphism
24	`T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} ≅ H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`, the resulting smoothness of dimension `g` at
25	the origin (hence everywhere by translation), properness inherited from the
26	projectivity of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` for `C/k` geometrically normal, and the assembly
27	into an abelian variety in the sense of Milne §I.1.
28	
29	## Status (iter-193 NEW file-skeleton)
30	
31	Each of the five blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
32	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
33	`Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`) with a `sorry` body. The bodies are
34	iter-194+ work; iter-193's mandate is the mechanical scaffold only.
35	
36	The 5 pinned declarations are:
37	
38	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (theorem, A.3.iii) — the
39	   canonical isomorphism `T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} ≅ H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` (Kleiman §5
40	   Thm.~`thm:tgtsp`). Packaged as an `AddEquiv` between the cotangent space
41	   `m_e/m_e²` at a `k`-rational identity-section point `e` and the project's
42	   first-cohomology `k`-module `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1`
43	   (from `Genus.lean`). The `k`-module structure on the cotangent space at a
44	   `k`-rational point is supplied as part of the existential bundle.
45	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.smooth` (theorem, A.3.iv) — `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is
46	   `Smooth` over `k`. Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Cor.~`cor:ch0`
47	   (characteristic-zero) + Ex.~`ex:jac` (curve case).
48	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.proper` (theorem, A.3.v) — `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is
49	   `IsProper` over `k`. Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (projectivity upgrade for
50	   geometrically normal `C/k`).
51	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible` (theorem, A.3.vi)
52	   — `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is `GeometricallyIrreducible` over `k`. Specialisation of
53	   `IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` (sibling) to
54	   `G = PicScheme C`. Kleiman §5 Prp.~`prp:pic0`.
55	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.isAbelianVariety` (theorem, A.3.vii) —
56	   `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety: `IsProper ∧ Smooth ∧
57	   GeometricallyIrreducible ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)`. Milne §I.1, p.~8
58	   (defining axioms) + Kleiman §5 Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`. Load-bearing A.3.vii gate
59	   of Route~A.
60	
61	## Note on type expressivity
62	
63	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
64	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
65	
66	- `tangentSpaceIso C` — existentially quantifies over a `k`-rational
67	  identity-section point `e : Spec k ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left` and a `k`-module
68	  structure on the cotangent space `m_e/m_e²` (the `k`-module structure
69	  arising via the `k`-algebra structure on the stalk at a `k`-rational point;
70	  for the file-skeleton this Module instance is bundled as part of the Σ').
71	  Bundles an `AddEquiv` between the cotangent space and the
72	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`-as-`k`-module. The body is iter-194+ work.
73	- `smooth C`, `proper C`, `geometricallyIrreducible C` — genuine `Prop`-valued
74	  statements on the structural morphism `(Pic0Scheme C).hom : Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶
75	  Spec k`, not tautological.
76	- `isAbelianVariety C` — assembles the conjunction of the four
77	  abelian-variety properties (proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible,
78	  group-object structure); not vacuous because each conjunct is a genuine

111	/-! ## §1. The `Pic0` namespace
112	
113	The blueprint chapter pins five Lean declarations under the
114	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0` namespace. Their underlying scheme
115	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is `Pic0Scheme C` from sibling `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`;
116	the declarations below collect the curve-specific abelian-variety facts
117	about `Pic0Scheme C` (tangent space at the identity, smoothness, properness,
118	geometric irreducibility, abelian-variety assembly). -/
119	
120	namespace Pic0
121	
122	/-- **Tangent space at the identity: `T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} ≅ H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`.**
123	
124	The Kleiman §5 Thm.~`thm:tgtsp` tangent-space isomorphism. For a smooth
125	proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the Zariski tangent space at the
126	identity of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is canonically isomorphic to the first sheaf
127	cohomology `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`.
128	
129	In Lean (skeleton form): we bundle existentially over a `k`-rational
130	identity-section point `e : Spec k ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left` and a `k`-module
131	structure on the cotangent space `m_e/m_e²` (the natural `k`-module structure
132	arising from the `k`-algebra structure on the stalk at a `k`-rational point;
133	for the file-skeleton this instance is supplied via Σ' to avoid hard-coding
134	a global instance prematurely). The substantive content is an `AddEquiv`
135	between the cotangent space and the project's `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` module
136	`Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1`.
137	
138	A k-LinearEquiv refinement (iter-194+) replaces the `AddEquiv` once the
139	`k`-module structure on the cotangent space is consistently threaded through
140	downstream consumers; the underlying additive group structure is enough for
141	the dimension corollary `dim_k T₀ Pic⁰ = dim_k H¹ = g(C)`. -/
142	theorem tangentSpaceIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
143	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
144	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
145	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
146	    Nonempty (Σ' (e : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left),
147	      IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace ((Pic0Scheme C).left.presheaf.stalk (e.base default))
148	        ≃+ Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
149	  sorry
150	
151	/-- **Smoothness of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.**
152	
153	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity component
154	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is smooth over `k` (Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Cor.~`cor:ch0` in
155	characteristic zero + Ex.~`ex:jac` for the curve case).
156	
157	The proof (iter-194+): smoothness at the identity propagates to smoothness
158	everywhere by translation (Pic⁰ is a `k`-group scheme, so multiplication by
159	any `λ ∈ Pic⁰(k̄)` is an automorphism). At the identity, smoothness reduces
160	to the inequality
161	`dim_0 Pic⁰_{C/k} ≤ dim_k T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} = dim_k H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = g(C)`
162	(`tangentSpaceIso`) becoming an equality. For a smooth proper curve this
163	equality holds: in characteristic zero by Cartier's theorem (every
164	`k`-group scheme is smooth), in positive characteristic by the curve-specific
165	vanishing `H²(C, 𝒪_C) = 0` (the obstruction to lifting tangent vectors). -/
166	theorem smooth {k : Type u} [Field k]
167	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
168	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
169	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
170	    Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom :=
171	  sorry
172	
173	/-- **Properness of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.**
174	
175	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity component
176	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is proper over `k` (Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p`; a smooth proper
177	curve is geometrically normal, hence the projectivity upgrade applies).
178	
179	The proof (iter-194+): `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is quasi-projective (Kleiman §5
180	Thm.~`th:qpp&p`, first conclusion); to upgrade to projective (hence proper)
181	we use the Chevalley–Rosenlicht structure theorem to reduce to ruling out
182	non-constant `k̄`-morphisms `𝔾_m → Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. The latter follows from
183	normality of `C ×_k 𝔾_m` and Hartshorne II Ex.~6.15 (invertible sheaves on
184	a normal integral scheme are sheaves of Cartier divisors). -/
185	theorem proper {k : Type u} [Field k]
186	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
187	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
188	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
189	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom :=
190	  sorry
191	
192	/-- **Geometric irreducibility of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.**
193	
194	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity component
195	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is geometrically irreducible over `k` (Kleiman §5 Prp.~`prp:pic0`,
196	specialisation of the abstract identity-component substrate
197	`IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` of
198	`Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` to `G = PicScheme C`).
199	
200	The proof (iter-194+): apply `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible`
201	to `G = PicScheme C` (locally of finite type by `FGAPicRepresentability`), and
202	identify `IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` with `Pic0Scheme C` via the
203	`Pic0Scheme` definition (sibling). -/
204	theorem geometricallyIrreducible {k : Type u} [Field k]
205	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
206	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
207	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
208	    GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom :=
209	  sorry
210	
211	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety (A.3.vii assembly).**
212	
213	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity
214	component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety over `k`: the underlying
215	`k`-scheme is smooth (`Pic0.smooth`), proper (`Pic0.proper`), and
216	geometrically irreducible (`Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible`), and it carries
217	a `k`-group-scheme structure inherited from `Pic_{C/k}` via the inclusion
218	`Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}`
219	(`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism`). Commutativity is
220	automatic (Milne §I.1, Cor.~1.4).
221	
222	Milne §I.1, p.~8: "A complete connected group variety is called an abelian
223	variety." This is the load-bearing A.3.vii gate of Route~A for
224	`thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness`.
225	
226	The proof (iter-194+) assembles the four conjuncts: `proper` (this file),
227	`smooth` (this file), `geometricallyIrreducible` (this file), and the
228	`Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))` slot, the latter from
229	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism` applied to
230	`G = PicScheme C` (sibling `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`). -/
231	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
232	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
233	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
234	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
235	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
236	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
237	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
238	  sorry
239	
240	end Pic0
241	
242	end Scheme
243	
244	end AlgebraicGeometry

11	# The identity component of the Picard scheme (A.3)
12	
13	This file is the **A.3** sub-build chapter for the project's positive-genus arm
14	of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It scaffolds the abstract identity-component
15	substrate for a `k`-group scheme locally of finite type and specialises it to
16	`G = Pic_{C/k}`, packaging:
17	
18	1. the open-and-closed subgroup-scheme structure of the identity component,
19	2. the degree map `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`,
20	3. the abelian-variety identification of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (the Jacobian variety
21	   of `C` when `C/k` is a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of
22	   positive genus).
23	
24	## Status (iter-185 NEW file-skeleton)
25	
26	This file is the **iter-185 IdentityComponent** file-skeleton (NEW lane,
27	blueprint-reviewer HARD GATE cleared for the iter-184 plan-phase chapter
28	`Picard_IdentityComponent.tex`). Each of the five blueprint-pinned
29	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
30	`\lean{...}` pin in `Picard_IdentityComponent.tex`) with a `sorry` body. The
31	bodies are iter-186+ work; iter-185's mandate is the mechanical scaffold only.
32	
33	The 5 pinned declarations are:
34	
35	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.IdentityComponent` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
36	   **identity component** `G^0` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite
37	   type, as a `k`-scheme (an `Over (Spec k)`-object). Abstract substrate
38	   reusable outside the Picard context; not yet in Mathlib.
39	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`
40	   (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the bundled statement that `G^0` is an open and
41	   closed subscheme of `G` via an open immersion `G^0 ↪ G`.
42	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0Scheme` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **identity
43	   component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`, obtained by applying
44	   `IdentityComponent` to `G = PicScheme C`.
45	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.degree` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **degree
46	   map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`, extracting the leading coefficient of the
47	   Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf relative to a fixed
48	   degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`.
49	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0Scheme.isAbelianVariety` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
50	   — the **abelian-variety identification** of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`: smooth, proper,
51	   geometrically irreducible `k`-group scheme of dimension `g(C)` --- the
52	   Jacobian variety of `C`.

54	## Note on type expressivity
55	
56	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
57	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
58	
59	- `IdentityComponent G : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a `k`-scheme, not the
60	  tautological `G` itself.
61	- `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme G` — asserts the *existence* of an
62	  open immersion morphism `IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` whose underlying map of
63	  schemes is both an open and a closed immersion (clopen subscheme).
64	- `Pic0Scheme C : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a `k`-scheme.
65	- `PicScheme.degree C : (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ` —

67	- `Pic0Scheme.isAbelianVariety C` — asserts the conjunction of the four

94	/-! ## §1. The identity component of a group scheme — abstract substrate
95	
96	The identity component is an abstract feature of `k`-group schemes locally of
97	finite type. We package the definition + open-and-closed-subgroup-scheme
98	structure in the `GroupScheme` namespace so they are reusable outside the
99	Picard context.
100	
101	In this project, a "`k`-group scheme" is encoded as an
102	`Over (Spec (.of k))`-object `G` carrying a `[GrpObj G]` instance (the
103	group-object structure in the over-category). The locally-of-finite-type
104	hypothesis is the `[LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]` instance on the structural
105	morphism to the base.
106	
107	Blueprint references: `def:identity_component_group_scheme` +
108	`thm:identity_component_open_subgroup` (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`). -/
109	
110	namespace GroupScheme
111	
112	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, the set of
113	connected components is finite.
114	
115	Proof: the map `irreducibleComponents α → ConnectedComponents α` sending each
116	irreducible component `C` to the connected component of an arbitrary chosen
117	point of `C` is well-defined (because `C` is preconnected, hence contained in
118	a single connected component) and surjective (because every point lies in
119	some irreducible component, namely `irreducibleComponent x`, which is a
120	subset of `connectedComponent x`). The source is finite by
121	`TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`, so the
122	target is finite by `Finite.of_surjective`. -/
123	private lemma noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
124	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α] :
125	    Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := by
126	  classical
127	  have hfin : (irreducibleComponents α).Finite :=
128	    TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents
129	  haveI : Finite ↥(irreducibleComponents α) := hfin.to_subtype
130	  refine Finite.of_surjective
131	    (fun C : ↥(irreducibleComponents α) =>
132	      (ConnectedComponents.mk : α → ConnectedComponents α)
133	        ((C.property : Maximal IsIrreducible C.val).prop.nonempty.some)) ?_
134	  intro c
135	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := ConnectedComponents.surjective_coe c
136	  refine ⟨⟨irreducibleComponent x, irreducibleComponent_mem_irreducibleComponents x⟩, ?_⟩
137	  rw [ConnectedComponents.coe_eq_coe]
138	  refine (connectedComponent_eq ?_).symm
139	  exact irreducibleComponent_subset_connectedComponent
140	    (isIrreducible_irreducibleComponent (x := x)).nonempty.some_mem
141	
142	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, each
143	connected component is open.
144	
145	Proof: `ConnectedComponents α` is totally disconnected
146	(`ConnectedComponents.totallyDisconnectedSpace`), hence T1
147	(`TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space`), and finite by
148	`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`. A finite T1 space is discrete
149	(`Finite.instDiscreteTopology`), so the singleton
150	`{ConnectedComponents.mk x}` is open in the quotient. The preimage of this
151	singleton under the continuous quotient map `ConnectedComponents.mk` is
152	`connectedComponent x` (`connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`), which is
153	therefore open. -/
154	private lemma noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent
155	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α]
156	    (x : α) :
157	    IsOpen (connectedComponent x) := by
158	  haveI : Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
159	  haveI : DiscreteTopology (ConnectedComponents α) := inferInstance
160	  have h := (isOpen_discrete
161	    ({(ConnectedComponents.mk x : ConnectedComponents α)} : Set _)).preimage
162	    ConnectedComponents.continuous_coe
163	  rwa [connectedComponents_preimage_singleton] at h
164	
165	/-- Helper (iter-188): the **`LocallyConnectedSpace` instance** for the
166	underlying topological space of a `k`-scheme `G.left` whose structural
167	morphism `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec k` is locally of finite type.
168	
169	The substantive content is EGA I 6.1.9: a locally Noetherian topological
170	space has open connected components — equivalently, is locally connected.
171	Pushed through the chain: `Spec k` is Noetherian (a field has Noetherian
172	spectrum); `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom + IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k) ⟹
173	IsLocallyNoetherian G.left` (Mathlib's
174	`AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); and the
175	implication `IsLocallyNoetherian X ⟹ LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat`
176	is the iter-188 project-side helper (`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`
177	+ `noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent`).
178	
179	The classical proof: each point `y ∈ G.left` has an open affine
180	neighbourhood `W = Spec R` with `R` Noetherian, hence `|W|` is a Noetherian
181	topological space; in a Noetherian space, each `connectedComponent y` is
182	clopen (finite irreducible components ⟹ finite connected components ⟹
183	finite T1 quotient ⟹ discrete quotient ⟹ singletons clopen in quotient ⟹
184	preimages clopen). Pulled back along the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left`,
185	this gives an open connected neighbourhood of `y` inside any open `F`
186	containing `y`. -/
187	private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
188	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
189	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
190	    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
191	    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left := by
192	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian G.left := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian G.hom
193	  rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected]
194	  intro x F hF
195	  -- Extract an open neighbourhood `T ⊆ F` of `x`.
196	  obtain ⟨T, hTF, hT, hxT⟩ := mem_nhds_iff.mp hF
197	  -- Find an affine open `W ⊆ T` with `x ∈ W`.
198	  obtain ⟨W, hW, hxW, hWT⟩ :=
199	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := G.left) (U := ⟨T, hT⟩) hxT
200	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(G.left, W) :=
201	    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨W, hW⟩
202	  haveI hNoethW : TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace ↥W :=
203	    noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen W hW
204	  -- Inside `W` (Noetherian) the connected component of `x` is open; its image
205	  -- under the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left` is the connected open neighbourhood
206	  -- we want.
207	  let xW : ↥W := ⟨x, hxW⟩
208	  refine ⟨(Subtype.val : ↥W → G.left) '' connectedComponent xW, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
209	  · rintro _ ⟨z, _, rfl⟩
210	    exact hTF (hWT z.2)
211	  · exact W.isOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val _
212	      (noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent xW)
213	  · exact ⟨xW, mem_connectedComponent, rfl⟩
214	  · exact isConnected_connectedComponent.image _ continuous_subtype_val.continuousOn
215	
216	/-- The image of the identity section `e : Spec k → G` (well-defined as a
217	single point of `|G|` because `Spec k` is a topological singleton). -/
218	private noncomputable def identitySectionPoint
219	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] : G.left :=
220	  ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left.base :
221	      ↥(Spec (.of k)) → G.left) (default : Spec (.of k))
222	
223	/-- Helper (iter-186; iter-187 closed): the **clopen carrier `Set`** of
224	the identity component of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type,
225	packaged as a `G.left.Opens`. The carrier set is `connectedComponent x`
226	for `x = e(*)` the image of the identity section
227	(`identitySectionPoint G`); openness is `isOpen_connectedComponent` which
228	needs the `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` instance above
229	(EGA I 6.1.9). -/
230	private noncomputable def identityComponentCarrier {k : Type u} [Field k]
231	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
232	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
233	    G.left.Opens :=
234	  ⟨connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G), isOpen_connectedComponent⟩
235	
236	/-- The **identity component** `G^0` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
237	finite type.
238	
239	Encoded as a `k`-scheme: an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`, carrying the
240	intended substantive identity "this is the connected component of `|G|`
241	containing the identity section `e`, equipped with the open-subscheme
242	structure inherited from `G`". The associated open immersion
243	`IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` is the content of
244	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`.
245	
246	iter-186 body: built from the `identityComponentCarrier G` helper — the
247	open subscheme of `G` whose underlying topological space is the connected
248	component of `|G|` through the image of the identity section. The
249	structure morphism is the inherited `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`.
250	The substantive content (the actual carrier `Set`, plus its openness from
251	EGA I 6.1.9: locally Noetherian spaces have open connected components)
252	lives in the typed-sorry body of `identityComponentCarrier`. -/
253	noncomputable def IdentityComponent {k : Type u} [Field k]
254	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
255	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
256	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
257	  Over.mk ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
258	
259	/-- **The identity component is an open and closed subgroup scheme.**
260	
261	The bundled statement of Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3): the identity
262	component `G^0 = IdentityComponent G` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
263	finite type comes with a morphism `IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` (in
264	`Over (Spec (.of k))`) whose underlying scheme morphism is both an open
265	immersion and a closed immersion (i.e. the inclusion of a clopen
266	subscheme).
267	
268	The full Kleiman conclusion also packages the group-subscheme property (the
269	inclusion is a homomorphism of `k`-group schemes), finite-type-ness over `k`
270	(`LocallyOfFiniteType` + quasi-compactness), geometric irreducibility, and
271	base-change-commutation. Those refinements live as separate instances /
272	follow-up lemmas in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the clopen
273	open-immersion conclusion as a Nonempty-witness.
274	
275	iter-186 body: the inclusion morphism is `Over.homMk (identityComponentCarrier G).ι`,
276	with the over-category compatibility holding by definition of
277	`IdentityComponent G`. The `.left` of this morphism is
278	`(identityComponentCarrier G).ι`, an open immersion by the global
279	`Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι` instance. For the closed-immersion
280	half we apply `IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion` to the open immersion
281	and reduce to `IsClosed (↑(identityComponentCarrier G) : Set _)`. The
282	remaining inline `sorry` is the substantive content: the carrier of
283	`identityComponentCarrier G` is closed, by "closure of a connected subspace
284	is connected" applied to the connected component through the image of the
285	identity section. -/
286	theorem IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
287	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
288	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
289	    Nonempty {f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G //
290	        IsOpenImmersion f.left ∧ IsClosedImmersion f.left} := by
291	  -- Over-category compatibility for the underlying open immersion.
292	  have hcomp : (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
293	    simp [IdentityComponent]
294	  -- The over-morphism witness.
295	  let f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G :=
296	    Over.homMk (U := IdentityComponent G) (V := G) (identityComponentCarrier G).ι hcomp
297	  refine ⟨⟨f, ?_, ?_⟩⟩
298	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` (definitionally; `Over.homMk_left` simp).
299	    -- An open immersion by `Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι`.
300	    change IsOpenImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
301	    infer_instance
302	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` is a preimmersion (open immersion ⟹
303	    -- immersion ⟹ preimmersion). Its range is the carrier set (`Scheme.Opens.range_ι`),
304	    -- and closure of that connected subset is itself (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3):
305	    -- the connected component through the identity is closed in `|G|`).
306	    change IsClosedImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
307	    apply IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion
308	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
309	    -- The carrier set is `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`, which is
310	    -- closed by `isClopen_connectedComponent.1` (with the
311	    -- `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` instance providing the closedness
312	    -- half of EGA I 6.1.9: connected components of a locally Noetherian topological
313	    -- space are clopen).
314	    change IsClosed (connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G))
315	    exact isClopen_connectedComponent.1
316	
317	/-! ### iter-192 Lane A.3.i: identity-component substrate
318	
319	Per `analogies/lane-a3i-isconnected-prod.md`, the substrate for the
320	group-structure inheritance and base-change-commutation arguments is
321	Stacks Tag 04KU / EGA IV₂ 4.5.14 (a connected `k`-scheme with a
322	`k`-rational section is geometrically connected) combined with Mathlib's
323	`ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` instance for
324	`[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f] [ConnectedSpace Y]`.
325	
326	This iter (Lane A.3.i): we add the AXIOM-CLEAN
327	`identityComponentCarrier_connectedSpace` helper below (the carrier is
328	connected by construction). The full Stacks 04KU bridge
329	"`ConnectedSpace X` + section ⟹ `GeometricallyConnected f`" requires
330	the Mathlib substrate "`X` connected with `k` algebraically closed in
331	`Γ(X, 𝒪_X)` ⟹ `X` geometrically connected over `k`" (Stacks 037Q +
332	04KU), not yet available in Mathlib at SHA b80f227 — pending that
333	Mathlib lemma, the geometric-connectedness step lives as a residual
334	sorry inside the downstream theorems
335	(`isSubgroupHomomorphism`, `baseChangeIso`, `isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible`).
336	
337	Below: `baseChangeIso` partially closes via
338	`CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd` (iter-192 axiom-clean closure
339	of 2 of the 3 conjuncts; the third conjunct, the iso of identity-component
340	constructions, remains sorry pending the Stacks 04KU substrate). -/
341	
342	/-- Helper (iter-192 Lane A.3.i, axiom-clean): the **identity component
343	carrier** has connected underlying topological space.
344	
345	The carrier is defined as `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`
346	in `|G|` (a `G.left.Opens`), and its subspace topology coincides with the
347	open-subscheme topology; the subspace is connected because the connected
348	component is preconnected (Mathlib's `isPreconnected_connectedComponent`)

382	/-- **Stacks 04KU / EGA IV₂ 4.5.14**: a connected `k`-scheme with a `k`-rational
383	section is geometrically connected.
384	
385	Given a morphism `f : X ⟶ Spec k` from a `ConnectedSpace`-typed scheme `X`
386	admitting a section `s : Spec k ⟶ X` (i.e. `s ≫ f = 𝟙`), the morphism `f` is
387	geometrically connected: for any field extension `K/k`, the pullback
388	`X ×_{Spec k} Spec K` is connected.
389

391	typed `sorry` to enable downstream consumers (sanctioned temporary sorry-count
392	increase). The full body is Stacks 04KV: a hypothetical clopen partition
393	`X_K = U ⊔ V` would, via the base-changed section `s_K = s ≫ (pullback π s)⁻¹`
394	say `K`-rational point landing in exactly one of `U, V`; the image of the
395	chosen piece under the projection `X_K → X` would then be a clopen subset
396	of `X` whose disjointness from the image of the other piece contradicts

399	axiom-clean closure this iter.

402	a `k`-scheme `X` is geometrically connected iff `X` is connected AND the

404	the pullback `s^* : Γ(X, 𝒪_X) → k` is a `k`-algebra retraction; any subfield
405	`F ⊆ Γ(X, 𝒪_X)` containing `k` maps injectively into `k` (as `F` is a field),
406	forcing `F = k`. Combined with `ConnectedSpace X`, gives the conclusion.

433	  --     `X ×_k Spec k'` is connected. This is the descent of geometric
434	  --     connectedness from arbitrary extensions to finite separable ones; it
435	  --     uses Galois-fixed-point arguments on `Spec(k' ⊗_k K)` for `K`

597	/-- **The identity component is of finite type and geometrically irreducible.**
598	
599	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (c): the open subgroup `G^0` of
600	a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type is itself
601	locally-of-finite-type-plus-quasi-compact (i.e., of finite type) over `k`,
602	and is geometrically irreducible. The proof reduces (after base change to
603	`\bar k`) to picking a nonempty smooth affine open subset `U ⊆ G^0`; the
604	translates `hg⁻¹U` give an open cover of `G^0` by smooth, hence irreducible,
605	neighbourhoods, so `G^0` is locally irreducible at every closed point; with
606	connectedness this gives irreducibility globally (EGA I 6.1.10). The image
607	`α(U × U) = G^0` is the image of the affine, hence quasi-compact, scheme
608	`U × U`, so `G^0` is quasi-compact.

664	theorem IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
665	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
666	    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra k K] :
667	    let φ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) :=
668	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))
669	    let G_K : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of K)) :=
670	      Over.mk (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ)
671	    Nonempty (Σ' (_grpInst : GrpObj G_K)
672	                 (_locFTInst : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom),
673	      (IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅
674	        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ) := by
675	  intro φ G_K
676	  -- `GrpObj G_K` via the category-theoretic base-change lemma
677	  -- `CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd`. The instance bridge
678	  -- `[GrpObj G] ⟹ [GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom)]` is by defeq (`G = Over.mk G.hom`
679	  -- when `G` is an over-category object whose right component is the unique
680	  -- inhabitant of `Discrete PUnit`).
681	  haveI hG : GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom) := ‹GrpObj G›
682	  haveI hGK_grp : GrpObj G_K := CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd
683	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom`: `G_K.hom = pullback.snd G.hom φ` is the
684	  -- base change of `G.hom` along `φ`; `LocallyOfFiniteType` is stable
685	  -- under base change (Mathlib instance).
686	  haveI hGK_lft : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom :=
687	    (inferInstance : LocallyOfFiniteType (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ))

689	  -- `identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` provides
690	  -- `GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom`, which combined with
691	  -- Mathlib's `[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f]
692	  -- [ConnectedSpace Y] ⟹ ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` instance gives
693	  -- `ConnectedSpace (pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ)` (the connected-
694	  -- pullback substrate). The iso construction proceeds by:
695	  -- 1. Build an open immersion `e : pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ ⟶
696	  --    G_K.left` via `pullback.map _ _ _ _ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
697	  --    (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) ...`, open-immersion status from
698	  --    `Scheme.pullback_map_isOpenImmersion` (i₁ = ι OI; i₂ = 𝟙 OI;
699	  --    i₃ = 𝟙 mono).

702	  -- 3. Apply `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` to get the iso between

704	  --    `(identityComponentCarrier G_K).toScheme = (IdentityComponent G_K).left`.
705	  -- Substantive content of step 2 (`~80-100 LOC`) deferred to iter-194+.
706	  refine ⟨⟨hGK_grp, hGK_lft, ?_⟩⟩
707	  sorry

711	/-! ## §2. The identity component of the Picard scheme
712	
713	We specialise the abstract identity-component substrate to
714	`G = PicScheme C`, the Picard scheme of a smooth proper geometrically
715	integral curve `C/k` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`).

720	namespace Scheme

722	/-- The **identity component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.
723	
724	Defined as the identity component
725	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` of the Picard scheme
726	`Pic_{C/k}` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`,
727	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`). By
728	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`, `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an
729	open and closed subgroup scheme of `Pic_{C/k}` of finite type over `k`,
730	geometrically irreducible, and its formation commutes with extension of the
731	base field.
732	
733	iter-186+: the body unwinds to `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`
734	once the `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` instance lands on
735	`PicScheme C` (it follows from the Kleiman §4 structure: `Pic_{C/k}` is
736	locally of finite type as the disjoint union of open quasi-projective
737	`k`-subschemes). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
738	noncomputable def Pic0Scheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
739	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
740	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
741	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
742	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
743	  sorry
744	
745	/-! ## §3. The degree map
746	
747	The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
748	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
749	`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
750	coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
751	relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
752	**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
753	
754	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/
755	
756	namespace PicScheme
757	
758	/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
759	
760	Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
761	`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
762	Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
763	to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
764	`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
765	coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
766	the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
767	relative Picard functor).
768	
769	The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
770	`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
771	group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
772	lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.
773	
774	iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
775	`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
776	Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
777	the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
778	`sorry`. -/
779	noncomputable def degree {k : Type u} [Field k]
780	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
781	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
782	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
783	    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
784	  sorry
785	
786	end PicScheme
787	
788	/-! ## §4. `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety
789	
790	The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
791	abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
792	this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
793	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
794	threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by
795	`AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP`.
796	
797	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
798	Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/
799	
800	namespace Pic0Scheme
801	
802	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**
803	
804	For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
805	over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
806	is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
807	irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
808	Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)
809	
810	The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
811	[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
812	`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.
813	
814	Dimension `g = g(C)`: not stated separately at the file-skeleton level
815	(it requires `dim_k (Pic0Scheme C).left = AlgebraicGeometry.genus C`, which
816	involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
817	follow-up.
818	
819	iter-186+: the body assembles the four conjuncts from
820	- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of
821	  `PicScheme C`),
822	- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹
823	  `Pic⁰_{X/k}` quasi-projective; upgrade to projective when geom. normal,
824	  which holds for smooth proper curves of positive genus),
825	- Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Ex.~`ex:jac` (smoothness of `Pic_{X/k}` at the
826	  identity for smooth proper curves; hence smooth of dimension
827	  `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)` everywhere by uniform smoothness on the open
828	  identity component).
829	For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
830	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
831	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
832	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
833	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
834	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
835	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
836	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
837	  sorry
838	
839	/-- **Dimension of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals the genus of `C`.**
840	
841	Milne~§III.1, Rmk.~1.4(e): "The dimension of `J` is the genus of `C`".
842	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k` of genus
843	`g = g(C)`, the topological Krull dimension of the underlying scheme of
844	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals `g(C)`. By Kleiman~§5 Cor.~`cor:sm`, the inequality
845	`dim Pic_{C/k} ≤ dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` is always an equality at points where
846	`Pic_{C/k}` is smooth, and for smooth proper curves
847	(`SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom`) the identity component is smooth
848	(Kleiman~§5 Ex.~`ex:jac`), so the dimension equals `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)`
849	by `def:genus`. -/
850	theorem finrank_eq_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
851	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
852	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
853	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
854	    topologicalKrullDim (Pic0Scheme C).left = (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : WithBot ℕ∞) :=
855	  sorry
856	
857	/-- **`k`-points of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` are the kernel of the degree map.**
858	
859	Milne~§III.1, p.~88: `Pic⁰(C)` is the group of isomorphism classes of
860	invertible sheaves of degree zero on `C`. For a smooth proper geometrically
861	integral curve `C/k`, a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` lies in the image of
862	the inclusion `Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}` (the inclusion of
863	`def:pic_zero_subscheme`, packaged here via the existence of the
864	inclusion morphism) if and only if `degree C λ = 0`.
865	
866	The statement-level pin packages two pieces: existence of the inclusion
867	morphism `Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶ Pic_{C/k}` (extracted from
868	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` once `PicScheme C` has the
869	`GrpObj` + `LocallyOfFiniteType` instances), together with the
870	characterisation of `k`-points factoring through it as those with degree
871	zero. -/
872	theorem kPoints_iff_kerDegree {k : Type u} [Field k]
873	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
874	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
875	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
876	    Nonempty (Σ' (inc : (Pic0Scheme C).left ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
877	      ∀ (lambda : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
878	        (∃ mu : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left, mu ≫ inc = lambda) ↔
879	          PicScheme.degree C lambda = 0) :=
880	  sorry
881	
882	end Pic0Scheme
883	
884	end Scheme
885	
886	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	# Relative spectrum of a quasi-coherent sheaf of algebras (A.1.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the relative-spectrum
13	functor `Spec_X(𝒜) : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Sch/X` used by the relative Picard functor on a
14	product `C ×_k T`.
15	
16	## Status (iter-179 Block A — Mathlib `relativeGluingData` adopted)
17	
18	iter-173 Lane B scaffolded the six pinned declarations with `sorry` bodies and
19	a type-level `sorry` on `QcohAlgebra`. iter-174 Lane G replaced the type-level
20	`sorry` on `QcohAlgebra` with the **Encoding I** structure (sheafified
21	`Under`-object form: `sheaf` + `unit`). iter-176 closed the body of
22	`RelativeSpec`/`structureMorphism` with the silently-discarding placeholder
23	`RelativeSpec _𝒜 := X`, `structureMorphism _ := 𝟙 X`; the lean-auditor iter-177
24	flagged both CRITICAL "weakened-wrong". The iter-178 mathlib-analogist consult
25	(`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`) identified that **Mathlib already ships
26	the construction** under a different name —

29	
30	**iter-179 Block A** lands the carrier upgrade: `QcohAlgebra` gains the

32	weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` and provably equivalent under

34	of `RelativeSpec` and `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` are now the Mathlib
35	canonical values `(relativeGluingData _).glued` and `.toBase`. Each of the
36	three downstream theorems (`UniversalProperty`, `affine_base_iff`,
37	`base_change`) previously discharged the trivialized goal against the

39	rewrites against `RelativeGluingData.cover` /

41	`SmallAffineZariski.isColimitCocone` (see consult Block B).

45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra` (**structure**, Block A iter-179)
46	   — a sheaf of commutative rings on `X`, an `O_X`-algebra unit

96	/-! ## §1. Quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_X`-algebras
97	
98	For a scheme `X`, a quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras is a sheaf of
99	`O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent. Stacks tag
100	01LL packages the notion as a sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ on $X$ taking values in
101	commutative rings together with a unit map from the structure sheaf
102	$\mathcal{O}_X$, plus the quasi-coherence requirement.
103	
104	iter-179 (Block A) packages this as a triple of (i) a sheaf of CommRings,
105	(ii) an `O_X`-algebra unit from the structure sheaf, and (iii) the
106	`Coequifibered` overlay (Stacks 01LL form): the affine restriction of the
107	unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. on every affine open `U` and section
108	`f`, the restriction-to-basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
109	This is the strictly-weaker, sheafified-tensor-free formulation that
110	Mathlib's `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes
111	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); it is
112	equivalent to the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` predicate under the
113	dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. See
114	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the iter-178 consult that
115	identified this idiom.
116	
117	Blueprint reference: `def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras` (Stacks 01LL,
118	situation-relative-spec). -/
119	
120	/-- A **quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras** (iter-179 Block A,
121	Mathlib-aligned form).
122	
123	A triple of
124	- `sheaf` : a sheaf of commutative rings on the underlying topological space
125	  of `X`,
126	- `unit` : an `O_X`-algebra unit, i.e. a morphism of sheaves of commutative
127	  rings `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` from the structure sheaf to the carrier, and
128	- `coequifibered` : the Stacks-01LL form of quasi-coherence — every
129	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is the
130	  `IsLocalization.Away f` of `sheaf U`. This is the exact predicate
131	  consumed by `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
132	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
133	
134	The shape matches the input of Mathlib's relative-gluing construction
135	verbatim, so `RelativeSpec` is defined directly as
136	`(relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`. The
137	`NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly weaker than the full
138	sheaf-of-modules quasi-coherence predicate
139	`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent`, but equivalent under the dense-subsite
140	equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`, so no information is lost. See
141	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2 for the consult that
142	identified this carrier shape. -/
143	structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where
144	  /-- The underlying sheaf of commutative rings on `X`. -/
145	  sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace
146	  /-- The `O_X`-algebra unit `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` exhibiting `sheaf` as a sheaf
147	  of `O_X`-algebras. -/
148	  unit : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf
149	  /-- **Stacks 01LL quasi-coherence overlay (`Coequifibered` form)**: every
150	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
151	  Strictly weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` (which needs
152	  sheafified-tensor infrastructure not yet in Mathlib); equivalent under the
153	  dense-subsite equivalence `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. This is the
154	  exact predicate consumed by `AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
155	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); see
156	  `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2. -/
157	  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered
158	    (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)
159	
160	namespace RelativeSpec
161	
162	/-! ## §2. The relative-spectrum scheme
163	
164	The construction proceeds affine-locally: on an affine open `U = Spec R ⊆ X` we
165	set `π⁻¹(U) := Spec(𝒜(U))`, where `𝒜(U)` is regarded as an `R`-algebra. The local
166	pieces glue compatibly because `𝒜` is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
167	`𝒜(U) ⊗_R S ≅ 𝒜(V)` for `V = Spec S ⊆ U` gives an open immersion of the
168	corresponding Specs).
169	
170	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_exists` (Stacks 01LQ
171	lemma-glue-relative-spec). -/
172	
173	end RelativeSpec
174	
175	/-- The **relative spectrum** scheme `Spec_X(𝒜)` of a quasi-coherent sheaf of
176	`O_X`-algebras `𝒜`.
177	
178	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
179	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`
180	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:102`,
181	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`). The
182	`coequifibered` field of `QcohAlgebra` is precisely the Stacks-01LL form of
183	quasi-coherence that Mathlib's `relativeGluingData` consumes: the affine
184	restriction of the `O_X`-algebra unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. every
185	basic-open restriction is an `IsLocalization.Away`. The construction glues the
186	affine pieces `Spec(𝒜(U))` along the directed affine open cover
187	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover X` and is the exact Mathlib-aligned
188	template used in `Hom.normalization`
189	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:120`); see
190	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the consult that identified this
191	idiom. -/
192	noncomputable def RelativeSpec {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : Scheme.{u} :=
193	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued
194	
195	/-- The **structure morphism** `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum.
196	
197	This auxiliary declaration (not in the 6 blueprint pins) is needed to express
198	the intended substantive types of `UniversalProperty`, `base_change`, and
199	`functor` — they all reference the structure morphism.
200	
201	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
202	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase`
203	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:114`). The map is the colimit
204	descent of the natural transformation `Spec(𝒜(U)) → U` over the directed
205	affine open cover; see `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 3.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: implicit in `thm:relative_spec_exists`. -/
208	noncomputable def RelativeSpec.structureMorphism {X : Scheme.{u}}
209	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : X.RelativeSpec 𝒜 ⟶ X :=
210	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase
211	
212	/-! ### Note: `QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor (iter-179 Lane B helper)
213	
214	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
215	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
216	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
217	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
218	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
219	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
220	`coequifibered` field is the Mathlib-gap claim that pushforward along an
221	affine morphism preserves the Stacks-01LL Coequifibered overlay
222	(Stacks 01LR pullback compatibility): `q` is affine via
223	`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` applied to `UniversalProperty 𝒜`, so on
224	every affine `U ⊆ T` with section `f ∈ Γ(T, U)` we have
225	`Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(f))) = Γ(P, q⁻¹U)[1/q.app f]`, i.e. the basic-open restriction
226	of the pushforward is the relevant `IsLocalization.Away`. iter-180 Lane C
227	factors this into two named helpers
228	(`QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`,
229	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`) declared after the universal
230	property has landed; the definition itself is also placed after the
231	namespace so it can consume those helpers. -/
232	
233	namespace RelativeSpec
234	
235	/-! ## §3. Universal property — affine structure morphism
236	
237	The Stacks 01LQ universal property says `Spec_X(𝒜)` represents the functor
238	sending an `X`-scheme `g : T → X` to the set of `O_X`-algebra maps
239	`𝒜 → g_* O_T`. A direct structural consequence of representability is that the
240	structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is *affine* (Stacks 01LR
241	lemma-spec-properties, immediate corollary). For the iter-173 file-skeleton we
242	encode the universal property by this affine-morphism consequence — the
243	substantive content is the same up to body unfolding, and the type is
244	non-tautological.
245	
246	iter-174+: refine the signature to a `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy`
247	witness against the functor of `O_X`-algebra maps once `QcohAlgebra` is
248	unpacked and the Hom-set on algebras is available.
249	
250	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_univ` (Stacks 01LQ lemma-spec). -/
251	
252	/-- **Universal property of the relative spectrum (affine-structure form).**
253	
254	The structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum is affine.
255	This is the substantive consequence of the Stacks 01LQ representability
256	statement (an `X`-scheme is the relative spectrum of some quasi-coherent
257	algebra iff its structure morphism is affine).
258	
259	iter-174+: refine the type signature to the full Yoneda-bijection statement
260	`Hom_X(T, Spec_X(𝒜)) ≃ Hom_{O_X-alg}(𝒜, g_* O_T)` once `QcohAlgebra` is
261	unpacked and an `O_X`-algebra Hom-set is in scope. The current type is the
262	non-tautological structural consequence used downstream by `affine_base_iff`
263	and `base_change`. -/
264	theorem UniversalProperty {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
265	    IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := by
266	  -- Mathlib `relativeGluingData` builder; per `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
267	  -- Block B. We invoke `isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`: for each `x : X`
268	  -- pick an affine open `U ∋ x` (every `X` has such by `exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`),
269	  -- and identify the structure-morphism preimage of `U` with the range of the colimit
270	  -- inclusion of the affine fiber `Spec(𝒜(U))` (via
271	  -- `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`); affineness of the
272	  -- opens-range then follows from `isAffineOpen_opensRange` since the source is
273	  -- `Scheme.Spec` of `𝒜.sheaf.val.obj (op U)`, an affine scheme.
274	  apply isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen
275	  intro x
276	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hxU, _⟩ :=
277	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (U := ⊤) (Set.mem_univ x)
278	  refine ⟨U, hxU, hU, ?_⟩
279	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered
280	  let i : X.AffineZariskiSite := ⟨U, hU⟩
281	  change IsAffineOpen (d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U)
282	  have key : d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U = (d.cover.f i).opensRange := by
283	    have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι i
284	    simpa [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
285	  rw [key]
286	  have : IsAffine (d.cover.X i) := by
287	    change IsAffine (Scheme.Spec.obj _)
288	    infer_instance
289	  exact isAffineOpen_opensRange _
